vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The visual data indicates a critical failure of the expected biome. P1 (String of Nickels), P2 (Mexican Mint), and P4 (Silver Guest) are not visible as living specimens. P3 (Pothos) is present but in a state of severe degradation.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No foliage detected in yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No foliage detected in black pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present: Pothos specimen identified by white rabbit anchor; however, it is severely desiccated/collapsed.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No foliage detected in black pot.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.P3: The Pothos is present but shows total loss of turgor pressure. The white rabbit anchor is visible, but the plant material is largely obscured or necrotic.
plant_audit.P4: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Soil appears uniformly dark and potentially hydrophobic or severely compacted.
biome_observations.debris: White particulate matter (likely eggshell fragments) is present in the P3 pot. A white cylindrical object (sensor/probe) is present in the yellow pot.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: Over the 5-day sequence, there is zero evidence of growth or recovery. The state of the biome has remained static and necrotic throughout the observation period.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of terminal decline. The lack of green biomass suggests that the specimens have either been removed or have succumbed to total desiccation/rot.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of eggshell fragments in the P3 pot is a notable intervention, though it has not improved the health of the Pothos.
vision_report.narrative_description: As a botanical observer, I must report that the current state of the desk garden is catastrophic. The registry provided does not match the physical reality of the pots, which are largely devoid of viable plant life. The Pothos (P3) is the only remaining specimen, and it is in a state of advanced collapse. No signs of active growth or recovery were observed over the last 5 days.
